CheckTitle: AI Agent CI/CD Pipeline Must Include Security Gates
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Supply Chain Security**. Validates that the CI/CD pipeline for each AI agent includes mandatory security gates covering secret scanning, static application security testing (SAST), and dependency vulnerability scanning. Without these gates, vulnerable or secret-leaking code can reach production.
Risk: A CI/CD pipeline without security gates is the most common path for introducing vulnerabilities into production. Hardcoded secrets, known-vulnerable dependencies, and insecure code patterns can be deployed undetected, providing attackers with persistent footholds.

Code.Other:
1. Add secret scanning (Gitleaks, Trufflehog, GitHub secret scanning) as a pre-merge gate.
2. Integrate SAST (Semgrep, Bandit, CodeQL) and fail the pipeline on high-severity findings.
3. Add dependency vulnerability scanning (pip-audit, npm audit, Snyk) as a required check.
4. Block merges that fail any security gate and require manual sign-off for exceptions.

Recommendation.Text: Add secret scanning, SAST, and dependency scanning as mandatory CI/CD gates that block merges on failure. Treat security findings as build-breaking defects.

Notes: ASPM-073 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
